ogTitle: Piggabeen vs Pillar Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Piggabeen and Pillar Valley.
heading: Piggabeen vs Pillar Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Piggabeen, New South Wales and Pillar Valley, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Piggabeen has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Pillar Valley (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Piggabeen or Pillar Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Piggabeen has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Pillar Valley scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Piggabeen scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Pillar Valley scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Piggabeen or Pillar Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Piggabeen has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
